metadata.title: Collective Certified Robustness Against Graph Injection Attacks

sections.title: Introduction
content: Deep Graph Learning (DGL) has emerged as a crucial technique across various domains. However, recent studies have exposed vulnerabilities in DGL models, such as susceptibility to graph injection attacks (GIAs). Existing research only provides sample-wise certificates by verifying each node independently, leading to very limited certifying performance. In this paper, we present the first collective certificate against GIAs, which certifies a set of target nodes simultaneously.

sections.title: Randomized Smoothing
content: For any node classifier f(·), its smoothed classifier g(·) is created by:
1. Node-aware Bi-Smoothing (Y. Lai. et. al. S&P2024): φ(G) = (φe(G), φn(G))
2. Majority vote: gv(G) := arg max P(fv(φ(G)) = y)
The goal of certified robustness is to verify: gv(G) = gv(G'), ∀ G' ∈ Bρ,τ(G).

sections.title: Collective Certificate Problem
content: Bρ,τ(G): the attacker can inject ρ malicious nodes, with τ malicious edges per node.
T: A set of target nodes.

sections.title: Collective Certificate via Optimization
content: p(Ev): the probability that the malicious messages is not intercepted.
The remaining |T| - M* nodes are certified robust.

sections.title: Sample-wise v.s. Collective Certificate
content: Sample-wise certificates essentially verify the prediction for a node one by one, assuming that the attacker can craft a different perturbed graph each time to attack every single node (Figure 1, top).
However, in reality, the attacker can only produce a single perturbed graph to simultaneously disrupt all predictions for a set of target nodes (Figure 1, bottom).

sections.title: Evaluation Results
content: With the same smoothing parameter, both proposed collective certificates achieve a higher certifiable radius (Figure 3), outperforming the sample-wise approach significantly when the ρ is large. For example, in the Citeseer dataset, when ρ = 140, our Collective-LP1 and Collective-LP2 have the certified ratios of 73.0%, and 81.2%, while sample-wise can certify 0.0% nodes.
